name: Tyrone Slothrop
description: American Army lieutenant stationed in London whose sexual encounters mysteriously correlate with V-2 rocket strikes. Becomes the subject of an elaborate intelligence investigation and eventually scatters across the Zone in a picaresque disintegration.

name: Captain Blicero
description: Sadistic SS officer obsessed with the V-2 rocket and transcendence through annihilation. Commands the firing of Rocket 00000 with its human payload. His reach extends backward through colonial Africa and forward to the novel's apocalyptic conclusion.

name: Pirate Prentice
description: British intelligence operative with the unusual psychic ability to host other people's fantasies. Opens the novel roasting bananas on a London rooftop. Later becomes a key figure in the Counterforce resistance.

name: Katje Borgesius
description: Dutch double agent who has survived Blicero's sadistic control and is deployed by the Allies to seduce and monitor Slothrop. Her loyalties remain permanently ambiguous as she moves between factions.

name: Roger Mexico
description: Statistician working for PISCES who maps V-2 strikes across London using Poisson distribution. His love affair with Jessica Swanlake provides the novel's most tender human relationship, threatened by the war's end.

name: Edward Pointsman
description: Pavlovian psychologist at The White Visitation, desperate to find a causal explanation for Slothrop's rocket-sexual map. Represents the Elect's need for control and deterministic order in a world that may be random.

name: Enzian
description: Leader of the Schwarzkommando — Herero rocket troops in the Zone. Former sexual captive of Blicero in colonial Africa. Seeks the Schwarzgerät as a path toward either tribal redemption or collective annihilation.

name: Vaslav Tchitcherine
description: Soviet intelligence officer sent to the Zone to recover rocket technology. Secretly driven to find and kill his half-brother Enzian, a quest that becomes the novel's ironic mirror of Slothrop's own scattering.

name: Franz Pökler
description: German rocket engineer who worked on the V-2 at Peenemünde. Kept compliant by the regime's promise of annual visits from his daughter Ilse — who may or may not be the same girl each year. His story is the novel's most devastating portrait of complicity.

name: Jessica Swanlake
description: Roger Mexico's wartime lover, engaged to Beaver. Their relationship embodies the fragile beauty possible only under the temporary democracy of the Blitz.

name: Geli Tripping
description: A young witch living in the Zone who becomes Tchitcherine's lover. Represents magic and intuition as alternatives to the rational systems of control that dominate the novel.

name: Gottfried
description: Blicero's young sexual captive and ultimately the human payload fired inside Rocket 00000. His arc is the novel's most extreme embodiment of sacrifice to the death-drive of the Elect.

name: Seaman Bodine
description: Raucous American sailor who appears across Pynchon's novels. In GR, he is a figure of anarchic resistance and low comedy who aids Slothrop in the Zone.

name: Laszlo Jamf
description: Mysterious chemist who allegedly conditioned the infant Slothrop using Imipolex G, creating the rocket-sexual response that drives the entire plot. Dead before the novel begins, yet his influence pervades everything.
